alternatives: Resolve concrete dependencies from feature implementations
Construct shared repositories inside registration closures
consequences: Application assembly remains possible without opening implementation APIs between features
Board registrations have stable dependency ownership
Re-activation does not multiply handlers or retain parent objects through back edges
context: Boardy composition must construct concrete implementations at the application edge while preserving feature visibility and lifecycle ownership.
decision: Restrict implementation imports and concrete construction to declared composition roots; keep required back edges weak, register flows in init, guard activation only when explicitly single-session, and store shared repositories on ModulePlugin.
migration: Move construction to declared roots, narrow public visibility, weaken back edges, register flows in init, and move shared repositories to ModulePlugin ownership.
title: Composition, DI, registration, and visibility
verification_impact: The final joined AI consistency review confirms composition, visibility, reference, flow-registration, activation, and repository ownership guidance is consistent.
